message:
Saturate remaining coeff*mono product in evaluate_quarantine_barrier

Apply the same saturating-multiply treatment to
`evaluate_quarantine_barrier` that the prior commit gave to
`evaluate_size_class_barrier_from_basis` and
`evaluate_provenance_barrier`: inside the per-degree monomial
accumulator, replace `i128::from(coeff) * mono / scale` with
`i128::from(coeff).saturating_mul(mono) / scale`.

`coeff` is an i64 polynomial coefficient and `mono` is an i128
monomial product whose magnitude grows with `degree`. A pathological
quarantine input can push either factor to the top of its range, and
the plain `*` panics under debug builds / wraps under release.
`saturating_mul` at the i128 stage pins the numerator into the i128
range so the subsequent `/ scale` division and the outer
`result.saturating_add(...)` keep the monotone "saturates toward
i128 bounds" guarantee that sibling barriers already provide.

`scale` is checked non-zero on the same line (`if scale != 0`) so
the division is unchanged, and `result` itself is still the i128
`saturating_add` accumulator — the only thing that moves is the
inner multiplication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
